Large-group & lodge cabins

Large-Group & Lodge Cabin Management in the Smokies

Big cabins are a different business than small ones. Haven runs your Smoky Mountain lodge for the reunions, weddings, and group trips that fill it, and keeps it compliant while doing it.

Book a Call About Your Property 865-263-7366
AirbnbSuperhost guest care
4.9★4,000+ Google reviews
Local teamBased in the Smokies since 2016
Listed & ranked across airbnbVrboBooking.comMarriottGoogle
Smoky Mountains

A large cabin is a group-travel business, not just a bigger listing

The Smokies are one of the best large-group destinations in the country. Family reunions, wedding parties, church and corporate retreats, and multi-family trips all come here looking for lodges that sleep a dozen guests or more, and those bookings command premium nightly rates. But a lodge is not simply a bigger version of a two-bedroom cabin. It books on a longer lead time, it lives or dies on group amenities like game rooms, theater rooms, and big kitchens, and it turns over with far more cleaning and wear than a small property.

Getting a large cabin right means pricing for group demand and events, marketing to trip organizers rather than couples, and running a heavy-duty housekeeping and maintenance operation that can reset a lodge perfectly between back-to-back stays. Handled well, the revenue is excellent. Handled casually, the reviews and the repair bills add up fast.

Haven manages large-group cabins across Gatlinburg, Pigeon Forge, Sevierville, Wears Valley, and the surrounding Smokies, with no hidden markups on cleaning or bookings.

How we help

What large-cabin owners get with Haven

Group and event pricing

A real revenue manager prices your lodge around group demand, holidays, and local events, and sets minimum-night strategy so peak dates earn what they should.

Marketing built for organizers

Listings and photography that sell the group experience: sleeping capacity, game and theater rooms, big kitchens, and gathering spaces that trip planners search for.

Heavy-turn housekeeping

Vetted local crews and inspected turnovers sized for large properties, so a 12-plus guest lodge is reset to a five-star standard on schedule, every time.

Occupancy-cap compliance

Pigeon Forge caps occupancy at 2 guests per bed, 2 beds per room, and 12 total. We market and price within the legal cap for your jurisdiction so you never risk the permit.

County permit tiers, handled

In unincorporated Sevier County, the STRU permit is $250 for homes sleeping 12 or fewer, and $250 plus $25 per additional occupant above that. We file it and keep it current.

Proactive maintenance

More guests and more systems mean more can break. Trusted local trades and preventive upkeep catch issues before a big group ever notices.

Full-service management

Everything handled, by one local team

Bookings and pricing, marketing, housekeeping, guest communication, maintenance, and compliance. You hand us the keys and we run the operation.

Bookings & calendar

We list, sync, and optimize your calendar across Airbnb, Vrbo, Booking.com, and direct, so every open night works hard.

Smart human pricing

A real revenue manager who knows this market adjusts your rates daily around demand, events, and seasonality.

Marketing & listings

Professional photography, written-to-convert listings, and multi-channel distribution that puts your cabin in front of more guests.

Housekeeping & turnovers

Vetted local cleaners, hotel-grade standards, and inspected turnovers between every stay.

24/7 guest communication

We answer guests fast, day or night, so small questions never become bad reviews.

Maintenance & contractors

We coordinate trusted local trades and handle issues before they reach you or your guests.

Direct deposit payouts

Clean monthly owner statements and reliable direct-deposit payouts. Reporting you can read without a spreadsheet chase.

Reviews & reputation

A guest-obsessed experience that earns the five-star reviews that drive future bookings and revenue.

What owners should know

Bigger cabins carry bigger rules. We keep you on the right side of them.

Occupancy is the first thing that trips up large-cabin owners. Pigeon Forge sets a hard limit of 12 guests per property, with a formula of two guests per bed and two beds per room, and marketing a cabin above its legal capacity is a fast way to lose a permit. Haven sets your listed occupancy to the legal maximum for your specific jurisdiction and prices accordingly.

Life safety scales with size too. In unincorporated Sevier County, properties that sleep more than 12 require fire sprinklers, on top of the standard interconnected smoke alarms, carbon monoxide alarms, tagged extinguishers, and egress requirements the county inspects annually. We prepare your lodge for inspection, stand in for it, and keep the permit current so a large cabin stays sellable and compliant.

Because these details differ by city and by whether a parcel is inside city limits or in the county, the first thing we do is confirm your property's jurisdiction, then build the pricing, marketing, and compliance plan around the rules that actually apply to it.

01

A local team

We are based right here in the Smoky Mountains, not in a call center three time zones away. When something needs a person on-site, we are minutes from your door.

02

Smart human pricing

A real revenue manager who knows this market sets your rates, not just an algorithm guessing from afar. That is how Haven owners run ahead of the market.

03

Guest-obsessed

A five-star guest experience is the engine of profit. Happy guests leave five-star reviews, rebook, and refer, which lifts your ranking and your rate.

Getting started

Switching to Haven is simple

We handle the heavy lifting so the transition is painless.

We evaluate your property

A local expert reviews your home, your market, and your numbers, then shows you what it can realistically earn under professional management.

We make profitable improvements

We recommend the high-return upgrades, photography, and amenities that move your listing up the rankings and lift your nightly rate.

You start earning more

We take over bookings, pricing, guests, cleaning, and maintenance. You watch the calendar fill and the deposits land, without the day-to-day work.

FAQ

Questions, answered

Yes. Large-group cabins are a core part of what we do. Haven manages lodges that sleep 12 or more across Gatlinburg, Pigeon Forge, Sevierville, Wears Valley, and the surrounding Smoky Mountains, handling group pricing, marketing, heavy-turn cleaning, maintenance, and compliance end to end.

Rules vary by jurisdiction. Pigeon Forge, for example, caps occupancy at 2 guests per bed, 2 beds per room, and 12 total per property. Marketing a cabin above its legal capacity risks the permit, so we set your listed occupancy to the legal maximum for your specific location and price around it.

Yes. In unincorporated Sevier County the STRU permit costs more for larger occupancy ($250 plus $25 per occupant above 12), and homes sleeping more than 12 require fire sprinklers in addition to the standard life-safety items. We handle the permit, prepare for the annual inspection, and keep everything current.

They command higher nightly rates and attract group bookings that smaller cabins cannot, but they also carry higher cleaning, maintenance, and compliance demands. The net result is strong when the operation is professional. We will build a realistic revenue estimate for your specific lodge.

With vetted local crews sized for large properties and an inspected, photo-verified turnover before every check-in. A 12-plus guest lodge needs a bigger, tighter housekeeping operation than a small cabin, and that is built into our management, not billed as a surprise.

Book a no-pressure call. We confirm your cabin's jurisdiction and occupancy rules, review the group-travel demand for your property, and show you what it can realistically earn under professional management.
